rationale.fidelity: Preserves a common ex ante friction choice, latent private type, strategic participation, type distribution and payoff information, and platform-payoff objective.
rationale.managerial_framing: Fully articulates deterrence versus benign participation, actor, timing, constraints, mechanism, counterfactual, and operational information needs.
rationale.scholarly_positioning: No scholarly positioning is requested or claimed; novelty is appropriately not asserted.
rationale.evidence_discipline: Labels strategic responses as assumptions and rejects observed type, universal safety improvement, prevalence, causal deterrence, and welfare claims.
rationale.prose_clarity: Well structured and precise, with slight verbosity and encoding noise.
rationale.overall: A complete strategic-participation framing that rigorously respects hidden type and avoids claiming friction always improves safety.
